data.title: Dallas Fed Manufacturing Index falls to -2.3 in April
data.description:
Texas Manufacturing Index (April): -2.3 vs. -0.2 in March, the Dallas Fed said on Monday.

Production index, a key measure of state manufacturing conditions, rose to 19.0 from 6.8 in the prior month.

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: Microsoft falls as OpenAI partnership evolves, says it will no longer pay revenue share
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) shares fell fractionally in early trading on Monday as the tech giant and OpenAI (OPENAI) said their partnership has continued to evolve, and OpenAI's license will become non-exclusive. 

“Today, we are announcing an amended agreement to simplify our partnership and the way we work together, grounded in flexibility, certainty and a focus on delivering the benefits of AI broadly,” Microsoft wrote in a statement on its website. “The greater predictability in the amended agreement strengthens our joint ability to build and operate AI platforms at scale while providing both companies the flexibility to pursue new opportunities.”

As part of the altered agreement, Microsoft will remain OpenAI's primary cloud partner, and OpenAI products will ship on Azure first. However, there is now a tweak that says if Microsoft “cannot and chooses not to support the necessary capabilities,” OpenAI can go elsewhere. 

In addition, OpenAI can bring all of its products to customers across any cloud provider, such as Amazon (AMZN) Web Services. Microsoft will also continue to have a license to use OpenAI's intellectual property for its models and products through 2032, but the license will now be non-exclusive. 

Amazon shares gave up earlier gains and fell 0.8% in late morning trading following the news.

As part of the amended deal, Microsoft will no longer pay a revenue share to OpenAI, though Microsoft will receive revenue share payments from OpenAI through 2030, regardless of its technological progress. 

Microsoft is still a major shareholder of OpenAI, the Windows maker added.

Seeking Alpha analyst Julia Ostian said the fact that an estimated 45% of Microsoft's AI backlog comes from OpenAI is a “major risk” for the Satya Nadella-led firm. However, it's likely any risks are already priced into Microsoft shares. 

“The market didn’t fully believe OpenAI would be able to pay for the previously agreed capacity, and for this reason, I don’t see this piece of news as majorly negative,” Ostian said via email.

Microsoft and OpenAI last amended their landmark agreement late in 2025, with a tweak in October to support its changing corporate structure.

(This story has been updated with analyst commentary.)

data.title: Avient promotes Giuseppe Di Salvo to CFO
data.description:
• Avient (AVNT) has promoted Giuseppe Di Salvo to the role of senior vice president and chief financial officer, effective June 1, 2026.
  • Di Salvo will succeed Jamie Beggs, who will be leaving Avient June 1, 2026 to pursue other opportunities. He brings 25 years of financial experience, including nearly 15 years at Avient, with extensive experience in investor relations, accounting, financial planning and analysis, and treasury.

data.title: Digital Realty cut to Hold at HSBC as strong AFFO growth already priced in
data.description:
Digital Realty Trust (DLR) stock slipped 1.6% in Monday morning trading after HSBC downgraded the stock to Hold from Buy. Analyst Phani Kanumuri sees it as unlikely that the data center REIT will be able to sustain the AFFO per share CAGR in the low teens that would merit a re-rating.

"Sustained AFFO per share growth in low teens could be a potential catalyst, but we currently see this as unlikely," Kanumuri wrote in a note to clients. The stock is trading at a premium compared with its average  historical multiple but now appears fair, considering it's on a path for strong AFFO growth.

For the sector, HSBC judges that the demand-supply equation is more favorable for traditional data center REITs like Digital Realty (DLR) and Equinix (EQIX). Demand continues to increase, fueled by agentic AI adoption, and new data center capacity remains constrained. Furthermore, increased prominence of inference and hybrid data centers is expected to strengthen traditional data centers competitive positioning, the analyst said.

Equinix (EQIX) remains a Buy at HSBC "as we see it a key  beneficiary of the inference phase given its retail leadership and expect accelerating AFFO growth as net impact from its more aggressive investments turns more  favorable in 2027-28," Kanumuri said.

HSBC's rating on Digital Realty (DLR) jibes with the SA Quant rating of Hold and diverges from the average SA Analyst rating  and average Wall Street rating of Buy.

data.title: Peter Schiff says, "Investors want yield now"—MSTR's BTC model cracking?
data.description:
Strategy (MSTR) announced on Monday via an SEC Form 8-K filing that it raised $255M by selling ~1.45M shares under its ATM program last week.

The firm used the entire amount to buy 3.27K Bitcoin (BTC-USD) at an average price of ~$77.9K per coin—but that's precisely what drew criticism from Peter Schiff, who questioned the sustainability of Strategy's accumulation.

The company now holds 818K+ BTC worth over $61B, continuing its aggressive accumulation strategy led by Michael Saylor. He also added that the firm has delivered a 9.6% BTC yield YTD 2026.

However, Peter Schiff, via its official X post, pushed back hard, arguing that Strategy’s (MSTR) funding model is weakening. He highlighted that in 2021, Michael Saylor raised money at 0% interest, while now the cost has jumped to ~11.5%, suggesting investors no longer want Bitcoin (BTC-USD) upside—they want guaranteed yield.

He also took aim at Michael Saylor’s 2025 bold prediction that BTC could hit $1M if Strategy (MSTR) accumulates 5% of the total supply. The firm currently holds ~3.9%, but Schiff argued that if further buying impacts prices the same way as before, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) could actually drop below ~$60K by the time that target is reached.

For now, markets seem unconvinced. Strategy (MSTR) stock is trading near ~$170, while Bitcoin (BTC-USD) has slipped from ~$79K to ~$77K recently, reflecting rising doubts around whether continuous buying alone can push prices higher—or if Peter Schiff’s caution is starting to play out.

The broader crypto market is also watching closely, with major assets like Ethereum (ETH-USD), Solana (SOL-USD), XRP (XRP-USD), BNB (BNB-USD), and more moving in sync with BTC.

data.title: Sandisk edges up ahead of Q3 earnings; Morgan Stanley increases estimates, target
data.description:
Sandisk (SNDK) will release its third quarter fiscal 2026 financial results post-market on Thursday, April 30, and Morgan Stanley increased its price target and earnings per share estimates ahead of the release.

Shares of Sandisk were up 5% during Monday morning market action. The stock's value has already quadrupled year to date as AI expenditures continue to drive up the demand and costs of NAND memory.

"Current third-party forecasts are for overall NAND ASPs to increase in the area of 90% in Q1 and 70-75% in Q2 with some relative outperformance in client SSDs (a segment of particular strength for SNDK vs. peers)," said Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Joseph Moore, in a Monday investor note.

Morgan Stanley increased its price target to $1,100 from $690 and retained its Overweight rating.

"We continue to like the stock, but near-term strength is understood; it may take awhile to believe in duration," Moore noted. "DRAM to us remains a more severe bottleneck to AI growth, but NAND is right there, and the longer-term prepayments should speak for themselves. And vs. DRAM, capex remains minimal, so supply is not a factor in catching up to demand."

For EPS, Morgan Stanley increased its 2026 outlook to $53.24 from $41.09, 2027 to $155.06 from $82.73, and 2028 to $134.13 from $91.92.

For the third quarter, a consensus estimate expects adjusted EPS of $14.55 and GAAP EPS of $13.82. Revenue for the quarter is estimated to total $4.72B. It was $1.7B during the same quarter last year.

data.title: Is Apple set for a dividend hike while buybacks dominate? SA analysts weigh in
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) is expected to announce another modest dividend increase when it reports quarterly results on Thursday, April 30, after market close, with Seeking Alpha analysts anticipating the tech giant will continue favoring share repurchases over larger cash payouts.

Seeking Alpha analyst Dividend Power expects Apple to increase its quarterly dividend rate by a penny to $0.27 per share, a 3.7% increase from the current $0.26 rate. The analyst explains that “despite the low yield, an extremely conservative payout ratio, and capacity for larger increases, the firm’s focus has been on share buybacks.”

“I expect the firm to stay true to form and announce another $100+ billion program, like in 2024 and 2025, leveraging its robust cash flow,” Dividend Power added.

Seeking Alpha analyst Harvesting Dividends also expects only a modest increase, citing Apple’s aggressive share repurchase strategy over the past several years.

"Apple's aggressively been buying back its stock over the last 5 years, most recently $20-25 billion per quarter.  This, along with an acceleration in R&D spending, leaves less free cash flow for dividend growth.  The company continues to prioritize share repurchases over dividend growth, which means investors can expect to see a modest dividend increase this year," Harvesting Dividends added.

Apple (AAPL) reported $15.4B in payments for dividends and dividend equivalents in the fourth quarter ended Sept. 27, 2025, compared with $15.2B a year earlier.

The tech giant raised its quarterly dividend by 4% last year to $0.26 per share and has increased its payout for 13 consecutive years. The firm also authorized an additional program to repurchase up to $100B of common stock at the time.

The stock currently yields about 0.38%, while its forward payout ratio remains near 13%, giving the company flexibility for future increases.

Still, with Apple continuing to generate massive cash flow and retire billions of dollars in stock each quarter, investors are likely to see capital returns remain tilted toward buybacks rather than meaningful dividend growth.

ETFs with notable exposure to Apple (AAPL): (GXPT), (AAPW), (FTEC), (VGT), (IYW), (TRUT), (MNZL), (TOPT), (CLCG), and (XLK).

Dividend ETFs: (VIG), (SCHD), (VYM), (DGRO), (SDY), (DVY), (IDV), (DGRW), (NOBL), (HDV), (RDVY), (VIGI), and (SDVY).

data.title: GSK subsidiary Tesaro, AnaptysBio provide update on contract claim litigation
data.description:
Tesaro, a subsidiary of GSK (GSK), and AnaptysBio (ANAB) provided an update on AnaptysBio litigation involving an anticipatory breach of contract claim.

GSK plc (GSK) subsidiary Tesaro said the Delaware Court of Chancery has granted a motion to dismiss AnaptysBio’s (ANAB) claim alleging anticipatory breach of contract.

The court’s ruling does not address the merits of the principal contractual dispute between the parties and has no impact on Tesaro's remaining claim against AnaptysBio for declaratory judgment, a statement on Monday said.

AnaptysBio (ANAB) in a statement on April 24, stated that the Delaware Chancery Court has dismissed Tesaro’s anticipatory breach of contract claim against Anaptys. "The ruling agrees with Anaptys’ position that it has never repudiated the Collaboration and Exclusive License Agreement (“Collaboration Agreement”) with Tesaro, a subsidiary of GSK, governing the development and commercialization of Jemperli," the company said.

The trial to adjudicate Anaptys’ contract claims and right to seek reversion of Jemperli against Tesaro/GSK is scheduled for July 14-17, 2026, AnaptysBio (ANAB) noted.

Tesaro initiated the litigation following allegations made by AnaptysBio (ANAB) that Tesaro has not fulfilled certain requirements of a 2014 license agreement, and that AnaptysBio intended to revoke Tesaro's licence for dostarlimab.

Jemperli, which is the brand name for dostarlimab, is currently approved in over 35 countries for use in certain endometrial cancers, the most common gynecologic cancer in the U.S.

Updates story to add AnaptysBio's statement.

data.title: Enphase Q1 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
Enphase Energy (ENPH) will report its results for the first quarter on Tuesday, after markets close.

Wall Street expects the company to post EPS of 45 cents, on revenue of $282.4M, representing a year-over-year decline of about 21%.

Enphase Energy’s quarter was characterized by improving analyst sentiment and a more constructive outlook on demand and margins, with several upgrades following stronger visibility into the business. The stock also gained on upbeat guidance and positive revenue expectations, as firms including Jefferies and others raised ratings on the back of improving fundamentals.

The company continued to emphasize longer-term growth opportunities in energy storage, including a targeted $2 billion battery retrofit market in the Netherlands and the rollout of next-generation batteries alongside AI-driven customer tools. At the same time, it undertook cost-cutting measures, including layoffs of around 160 employees as part of a broader restructuring effort.

Overall, the period reflected a mix of improving forward-looking confidence, strategic expansion in storage and software, and ongoing efforts to streamline operations.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, ENPH is rated Hold with an overall score of 3.12 out of 5, reflecting a B grade in terms of revisions, but it has an F in terms of growth.

A recent Seeking Alpha analysis said Enphase’s near-term performance remains challenged by weak demand in key regions and regulatory uncertainty, even as operational improvements and emerging growth drivers provide some support, pointing out that “even though the company is seeing revenue drops, it stays relatively stable on gross margins, given all the headwinds it faces.”

Over the past two years, ENPH has beaten EPS estimates 50% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 50% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen 14 upward revisions and three downward revisions, while revenue estimates have seen 14 upward revisions and three downward moves.

data.title: Americold Realty Trust, Lineage downgraded at Barclays on industry challenges
data.description:
Americold Realty Trust (COLD) and Lineage (LINE) were downgraded at Barclays over 'persistent challenges' that the cold storage industry faces.

LINE shares were trading 2.10% lower at $36.36 after the market opened on Monday, while COLD fell 1.87% to $12.59.

The investment bank lowered its rating on the stocks to Underweight from Overweight and Equal Weight on Americold and Lineage, respectively. Price targets were set at $12.83 and $37.14.

"Rising fertilizer costs add to the persistent challenges the cold storage industry faces: excess supply, GLP-1 adoption, food inflation, population deceleration, tariffs, diminished cattle herds, etc.," said analysts Brendan Lynch, Eileen Gao, and Annabelle Ayer.

"Our checks suggest leasing and transaction volume slowed in February and March due to the uncertainty," said the analysts.

"We see greater risk to 2027 consensus estimates, due to the protracted nature of the industry challenges. While we do not anticipate a guidance cut, we do think weak 1Q26 numbers could weigh on sentiment/trading multiples," said the research note.

Barclays noted that the downgrade did not result from a negative call on the management team.

Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system grade COLD as Hold, while SA authors see the stock as Buy. Meanwhile, sell-side analysts rate LINE as Hold, SA authors as Buy, and Quant as Sell.
